Arrived just before 6, 12th in line. Walked in with Bob Lubbehusen and Ed Warburton -- they saved a seat. She played one song using an all-white right-handed bass with an embedded working neon tube that had been built for Dusty Hill of ZZ Top.
VIP merch: last 2 LPs (most recent autographed), poster, lanyard. Hat signed by Jesse Williams (supporting act, not in Musicbrainz).
Photobomb photo w/ Shane Sanders and JST.
RHBS signed by Selwyn Birchwood and Regi Oliver; CD & sticker merch

Both artists played solo then invited the other up for part of the set. Amidon: acoustic guitar, banjo, fiddle. JXF: electric and acoustic. ~100 people. Sam mentioned folk artist parents Peter and Mary Alice (1970s). Hat questions from audience; one woman mistook Dan for NPR's Bob Boilen.

Don't Settle Tour. Stage right balcony. 3rd attempt to see Hansard. Prior Apr 2020 Glaspy headline ticket (pandemic cancellation). Glaspy plans new album + headline Fall 2026 tour. Hansard played electric and acoustic; took requests from fans on the rail; covered Gloria (Van Morrison) for encore shouting out Jose Andres. Hat autograph: Margaret Glaspy (supporting act).

Venue Address: 220 N Washington St, Falls Church, VA 22046
Notes / Memories: Impromptu show. First time seeing them since the switch from New York's Finest. Police Cars tribute bill. Did not stay for The Side Cars Band.
Closer To The Bone Tour. Saw Larkin Poe t-shirt and said you have good taste. Invited Charlie Owen from audience to sing Further on Down the Road (cover) before Big Sister's Radio. Signed CD.
RHBS autograph + signed photo from previous show.
Night Two; hometown show. Jennifer, Lary, and Bob in the audience. Collier called Lary by name before playing Icarus. RHBS autograph: Laura Chavez (at the bar pre-show; hat was signed 2024-09-08).

VIP: only 3 attendees. Sat across from Kanene Pipkin. Played 3 songs incl. Call to War. Talked about Cool Disco Dan shirt, the hat (Kanene claimed to remember it), and being mistaken for Bob Boilen — Zach mentioned hat in that context. Kanene's parents in the audience. Photo taken by Tyler (tour manager / occasional keys). Trio autographed portrait prints from 11/2024 show. Merch: lanyard + signed 2026 tour poster.

Power trio + Deanna Bogart (Tommy Castro's wife) on sax. During drum solo to end set one, Gabe went to Baskin Robbins for a cone and came back eating it. Set two brought up guitarist from the early show. Did not stay for set three. $5 tip jar.

Animals as Leaders: very metal, both guitarists play 8-string guitars, no traditional bassist. SatchVai Band: Joe Satriani & his drummer, Steve Vai and his rhythm guitarist, and a bass player who also did some vocals. Pure guitar virtuosity. Sat with Lary and Guinness record holder Mike Brown.
Misquoted Dan mid-set asking "how's that going?"; said "bless you" after a sneeze.
Fifth in line with Ed. Ran into Jackie and the band leaving the venue to find food; signed poster and t-shirt again, shared a laugh about them turning the wrong way. Discovery Series.

Attended with Lary and his brother, and Matt and Tijan Brown; Ed and Bob also in the house. Kingfish: new bass player in his four-piece (keyboards and drummer same). Gary five-piece: drummer, bass, keys, slide/rhythm guitar. Gary brought Kingfish out for a song in the main set — partially missed due to bathroom run.

Supporting Artist: Jobi Riccio / Fancy Hagood / Allison Russell / Emmylou Harris / I'm With Her / Margo Price / Lucius / Hayes Carll / Patty Griffin / Tommy Prine
Notes / Memories: Sold out show; first experience in the pit seats. Prine's original band + new keyboard/accordion player. Each artist played a Prine cover and an original. Lucius and I'm With Her new discoveries. Allison Russell debuted a new song. Checked Emmylou Harris, Margo Price, and Patty Griffin off the life list. Prine's son led the final set with group sing-along of Paradise. Multiple artist setlists — see issue #43.

Power trio band. Custom PRS guitar. PRS himself was in the house and came up to play slow blues. 11-year-old boy and a guy with a long beard each invited up for different songs. Played 3 acoustic tracks on a guitar she bought the day before in Richmond. She complimented the hat to the crowd before Sharp Dressed Man. Hat signed from stage; got the pick she used on Voodoo Child cover.
Left early around 9pm. 3rd show in 4 days and 5th in 8 — too tired and a standing show. Crowd vibe in the balcony was off kilter as well. All Good Now Pre Party.

Molly Tuttle opened, Daniel Donato closed — sets basically even length. Each came out to play on one track in the other's set. Molly's 5-piece was all-female rhythm section + 2 multi-instrumentalists (violin/mandolin/keys, banjo/lap steel). Daniel's 4-piece was the same: Mustang McGee on bass, Sugarleg on keys, and a drummer. Artist interaction: none. Bumped into the Berman family in the lounge. Philip Blumenthal texted from his lawn seat after spotting the hat. Attended with Lary.

Attended with Melanie; Lary met in the lounge. Allison Russell: same four-piece all-female band, debuted new songs (support set). Sarah McLachlan: six-piece band, all on vocals/multi-instruments except lead guitar; she played grand piano or acoustic rhythm guitar. 2-hour show.
No opener. Hat signed by Cary Ann Hearst backstage after the show.

Artist: Taj Mahal and the Phantom Blues Band
Notes / Memories: Billed as one act ("Taj Mahal and the Phantom Blues Band") — no separate opener. Counts as 2nd Times Seen for Taj Mahal. Elizabeth Cotten's great-granddaughter was in the audience; band played a tribute cover of "Freight Train" in her honor.

no refunds. Seven-piece band; two sets, mostly new album material (due end of July). Larry and Ed at center table; sat one table over with Matt, in front of Dave Barnes (lead guitar) and the keyboard player.

The Steepwater Band (Chicago, 4-piece, 2 guitars) opened; Robert Jon & The Wreck played as a 5-piece (keys plus 2 guitars). Henry James's lead guitar was phenomenal. Long show, no artist interaction. Jay Brooke was in the crowd and saw the hat.

Supporting Artist: Mavis Staples / The War and Treaty
Notes / Memories: Larry provided lounge access; sat two rows in front of us. Jennifer Timberlake was our seatmate. Short opening sets from The War and Treaty and Mavis Staples — Tonya Trotter (The War and Treaty) is from Oxon Hill and mentioned coming home; Mavis Staples, 87, still going strong. During the closing Orleans Avenue second line through the crowd, band members (bari sax and tenor sax, incl. Troy's nephew) stopped right at our seats to play a few bars — Troy hoisted his trombone before moving on.
